tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

{"total_count":5,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4215064141,"number":57840,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[ContainerServiceFleet] Docs: Add ManagedNamespace documentation and update CHANGELOG","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet` **Service directory:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/` **Triggered by:** PR #57267 (commit 22e0451be) by `@frantran` ## What Changed PR #57267 added `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource`, `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection`, and related models/data types representing the new `ManagedNamespace` resource under a Fleet. It also added scenario test coverage (`FleetCRUD.cs`) exercising CRUD operations for ManagedNamespace. The unreleased `1.2.0-beta.2` CHANGELOG section was not updated. ## Gaps Found - **CHANGELOG not updated**: The `1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)` section under `### Features Added` is empty despite ManagedNamespace support being added - **README Examples section is a stub**: The `## Examples` section only links to generic SDK samples; no concrete code examples or snippets exist for any Fleet resource types - **ManagedNamespace not mentioned in README**: The new `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource` type, its CRUD operations, and key concepts (DefaultResourceQuota, DefaultNetworkPolicy, etc.) are completely absent from the README - **No Samples test file**: There is no `tests/Samples/` directory with snippet-backed samples; the `## Examples` section cannot link to testable code examples
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the `1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased)` section to add the ManagedNamespace entry: ```markdown ## 1.2.0-beta.2 (Unreleased) ### Features Added - Added `ManagedNamespace` resource support (`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource`, `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection`, `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData`), enabling creation, update, deletion, and enumeration of managed namespaces within a Fleet. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets 1. **Create** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/tests/Samples/Sample1_HelloWorld.cs` Provide at minimum two snippet-backed samples: - `Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateFleet` – authenticate, create a Fleet resource - `Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateManagedNamespace` – create a ManagedNamespace under an existing Fleet Example structure: ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System; using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Core.TestFramework; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet; using Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Models; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_HelloWorld : SamplesBase { [Test] [SyncOnly] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task CreateFleetAndManagedNamespace() { #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateFleet ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync(""); ContainerServiceFleetCollection fleets = resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleets(); ContainerServiceFleetData fleetData = new ContainerServiceFleetData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation("eastus")); ArmOperation createOp = await fleets.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, "", fleetData); ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = createOp.Value; #endregion #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateManagedNamespace ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection namespaces = fleet.GetContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaces(); ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData nsData = new ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation("eastus")); ArmOperation nsOp = await namespaces.CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, "", nsData); ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource ns = nsOp.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created namespace: {ns.Data.Name}"); #endregion } } } ``` 2. **Update README** at `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md`: Replace the current `## Examples` section content with snippet placeholders: ```markdown ## Examples ### Create a Fleet ```C# Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateFleet ``` ### Create a ManagedNamespace ```C# Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateManagedNamespace ``` ``` ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md` currently has all required sections but needs the following improvements: | Section | Status | Action needed | |---|---|---| | Getting started > Install the package | ✅ Present | None | | Getting started > Prerequisites | ✅ Present | None | | Getting started > Authenticate the client | ✅ Present | None | | Key concepts | ⚠️ Generic link only | Add bullet points describing Fleet, FleetMember, ManagedNamespace, UpdateRun, Gate resource types | | Examples | ❌ No code | Add snippet placeholders as described above | | Troubleshooting | ✅ Present | None | | Next steps | ✅ Present | None | | Contributing | ✅ Present | None | For the **Key concepts** section, add a description along the lines of: ```markdown ## Key concepts - **Fleet** (`ContainerServiceFleetResource`): Top-level resource representing a group of AKS clusters managed together. - **FleetMember** (`ContainerServiceFleetMemberResource`): An AKS cluster enrolled in the Fleet. - **ManagedNamespace** (`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource`): A namespace propagated and managed across all Fleet member clusters, with configurable resource quotas and network policies. - **UpdateRun** (`ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRunResource`): A sequenced rollout of Kubernetes version upgrades across Fleet members. - **Gate** (`ContainerServiceFleetGateResource`): A signal-based gate that can pause an UpdateRun until a condition is satisfied. ``` ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 fleet` — injects snippet code from test files into README placeholders 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 fleet` — if any public types changed 5. `dotnet format sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Docs: Document new Gates and ManagedNamespace resources added in 2025-08-01-preview","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet` **Service directory:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/` **Triggered by:** PR #57844 (commit 698b910c) by `@azure-sdk` (co-authored by `@ftran`) ## What Changed PR #57844 upgraded `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet` to API version `2025-08-01-preview` (released as v1.2.0-beta.2), adding two new resource types: - `ContainerServiceFleetGateResource` / `ContainerServiceFleetGateCollection` — represents a `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/gates` resource for controlling fleet update progression - `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource` / `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection` — represents a `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/managednamespaces` resource for namespace management within a fleet ## Gaps Found - The README has no Fleet-specific **Key concepts** section — it only links to generic Azure SDK docs; there is no explanation of what a Fleet, Gate, or ManagedNamespace resource is - The README **Examples** section only links to a general code sample gallery; there are no inline or snippet-backed code examples showing how to use Fleet, Gate, or ManagedNamespace operations - No `tests/Samples/` directory exists — there are no test-backed snippet files at all for this package - The **CHANGELOG** entry for v1.2.0-beta.2 (`## 1.2.0-beta.2 (2026-04-06)`) only says "Upgraded api-version to '2025-08-01-preview'" and does not list the new resource types or describe what they enable
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the `## 1.2.0-beta.2 (2026-04-06)` entry to describe the newly added resource types: ```markdown ## 1.2.0-beta.2 (2026-04-06) ### Features Added - Upgraded api-version to `2025-08-01-preview`. - Added `ContainerServiceFleetGateResource` and `ContainerServiceFleetGateCollection` for managing fleet update gates (`Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/gates`). - Added `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource` and `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection` for managing namespaces within a fleet (`Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/managednamespaces`). ``` #### File 2: `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md` Replace the **Key concepts** section (currently a one-liner link) and the **Examples** section (currently only a gallery link) with substantive content. Replace the existing `## Key concepts` section with: ```markdown ## Key concepts The `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet` library maps to these top-level resource types in the `Microsoft.ContainerService` provider: | Resource class | ARM resource type | Description | |---|---|---| | `ContainerServiceFleetResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets` | A Fleet hub cluster used to manage a group of member AKS clusters | | `ContainerServiceFleetMemberResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/members` | An AKS cluster enrolled as a member of a Fleet | | `ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRunResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/updateRuns` | An orchestrated update run across member clusters | | `FleetUpdateStrategyResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/updateStrategies` | A reusable update strategy defining stage and group ordering | | `AutoUpgradeProfileResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/autoUpgradeProfiles` | Configuration for automatic Kubernetes version upgrades across member clusters | | `ContainerServiceFleetGateResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/gates` | A gate that can pause an update run until explicitly resolved *(preview)* | | `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/managednamespaces` | A namespace propagated and managed centrally across all member clusters *(preview)* | For background concepts, see the [Kubernetes Fleet Manager documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` Replace the existing `## Examples` section with: ```markdown ## Examples The following sections show code snippets for common scenarios. For more samples, see the [.NET Management Library Code Samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ### Create a Fleet ```C# Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateFleet ``` ``` ### Enumerate Fleet member clusters ```C# Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_ListFleetMembers ``` ``` ### Resolve a Fleet update gate ```C# Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_ResolveGate ``` ``` ### Create a managed namespace ```C# Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateManagedNamespace ``` ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets Create a new test/sample file at `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/tests/Samples/Sample1_ContainerServiceFleet.cs` with the following content. Wrap code that should appear in the README with `#region Snippet:` / `#endregion`. Wrap test-only setup/assertions with `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`. ```csharp // Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved. // Licensed under the MIT License. using System.Threading.Tasks; using Azure.Identity; using Azure.ResourceManager; using Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet; using Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Models; using Azure.ResourceManager.Resources; using NUnit.Framework; namespace Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet.Tests.Samples { public class Sample1_ContainerServiceFleet { [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task CreateFleet() { #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_CreateFleet ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); SubscriptionResource subscription = await client.GetDefaultSubscriptionAsync(); ResourceGroupResource resourceGroup = await subscription.GetResourceGroupAsync("myResourceGroup"); ContainerServiceFleetCollection fleets = resourceGroup.GetContainerServiceFleets(); ContainerServiceFleetData fleetData = new ContainerServiceFleetData(new Azure.Core.AzureLocation("eastus")); ArmOperation operation = await fleets.CreateOrUpdateAsync( WaitUntil.Completed, "myFleet", fleetData); ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = operation.Value; #endregion } [Test] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task ListFleetMembers() { #region Snippet:ContainerServiceFleet_ListFleetMembers ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); Docs: Add code examples and document new Gates and ManagedNamespace features","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet` **Service directory:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/` **Triggered by:** PR #57844 by `@frantran` ## What Changed PR #57844 generated version `1.2.0-beta.2` by upgrading the API version to `2025-08-01-preview`. This added two new resource types to the SDK: - **`ContainerServiceFleetGate` / `ContainerServiceFleetGateData` / `ContainerServiceFleetGateCollection`** — Gates support for fleet-level upgrade coordination - **`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespace` / `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData` / `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection`** — Managed Namespace support Combined with features from `1.2.0-beta.1` (AutoUpgrade profiles, FleetMember labels, TargetKubernetesVersion), the package now has substantially more API surface than what the README describes. ## Gaps Found - The README **Examples** section contains no inline code samples — it only links to `(aka.ms/redacted), which is a generic redirect. There is no package-specific example showing how to create a Fleet, add a member, trigger an update run, create a Gate, or manage a ManagedNamespace. - There are **no snippet placeholders** (`C# Snippet:`) in the README that would be populated by `Update-Snippets.ps1`. - There is **no `tests/Samples/` directory** with hand-crafted sample test files. The only samples are the auto-generated ones under `tests/Generated/Samples/`, which are not wired into the README. - The **Key concepts** section is a one-liner pointing to general SDK docs and says nothing specific to the Fleet service (e.g., what a Fleet is, what Members are, what Update Runs are, what Gates are, what Managed Namespaces are). - The new **Gates** and **ManagedNamespace** features added in this and the prior beta are not mentioned or demonstrated anywhere in the README.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Update `README.md` **File:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md` **Action:** Edit Replace the placeholder **Key concepts** section with a concise description of the Fleet service domain model: ```markdown ## Key concepts ### Fleet A `Fleet` resource (represented by `ContainerServiceFleetResource`) is the top-level management object that groups AKS member clusters for coordinated lifecycle management. ### Fleet Member A `ContainerServiceFleetMember` represents an AKS cluster enrolled in the fleet. Members can be labeled for targeted update strategies. ### Update Run A `ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRun` orchestrates rolling upgrades across member clusters in defined stages and groups. ### Update Strategy A `FleetUpdateStrategy` defines a reusable template of stages and groups for update runs. ### Auto Upgrade Profile An `AutoUpgradeProfile` automates Kubernetes version upgrades across fleet members based on release channel settings. ### Gate A `ContainerServiceFleetGate` is a pre/post-upgrade checkpoint that can pause an update run until external conditions are satisfied, enabling integration with deployment pipelines. ### Managed Namespace A `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespace` propagates a Kubernetes namespace (with RBAC, resource quotas, and network policies) across all fleet member clusters from the fleet hub. ``` Replace the placeholder **Examples** section content with snippet placeholders (see Step 2): ```markdown ## Examples The following examples demonstrate common operations with the Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager library. ### Create a Fleet ```C# Snippet:CreateFleet ``` ### Add a Fleet Member ```C# Snippet:AddFleetMember ``` ### Start an Update Run ```C# Snippet:CreateUpdateRun ``` ### Create a Gate ```C# Snippet:CreateGate ``` ### Create a Managed Namespace ```C# Snippet:CreateManagedNamespace ``` For more examples, see the [Azure SDK for .NET management library samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ``` > **Note:** The backtick fences above are abbreviated for clarity. ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = operation.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created Fleet: {fleet.Data.Name}"); #endregion } [Test] [SyncOnly] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task AddFleetMember() { #region Snippet:AddFleetMember ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); ResourceIdentifier fleetId = ContainerServiceFleetResource.CreateResourceIdentifier( "", "", ""); ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = client.GetContainerServiceFleetResource(fleetId); ContainerServiceFleetMemberCollection members = fleet.GetContainerServiceFleetMembers(); string aksClusterResourceId = "/subscriptions//resourceGroups//providers/Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters/"; ContainerServiceFleetMemberData memberData = new ContainerServiceFleetMemberData { ClusterResourceId = new ResourceIdentifier(aksClusterResourceId) }; ArmOperation operation = await members.CreateOrUpdateAsync( WaitUntil.Completed, "", memberData); ContainerServiceFleetMemberResource member = operation.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Added member: {member.Data.Name}"); #endregion } [Test] [SyncOnly] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task CreateUpdateRun() { #region Snippet:CreateUpdateRun ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); ResourceIdentifier fleetId = ContainerServiceFleetResource.CreateResourceIdentifier( "", "", ""); ContainerServiceFleetResource fleet = client.GetContainerServiceFleetResource(fleetId); ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRunData updateRunData = new ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRunData { ManagedClusterUpdate = new ContainerServiceFleetManagedClusterUpdate( new ContainerServiceFleetManagedClusterUpgradeSpec( ContainerServiceFleetManagedClusterUpgradeType.Full) { KubernetesVersion = "1.30" }) }; ArmOperation operation = await fleet .GetContainerServiceFleetUpdateRuns() .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, "", updateRunData); ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRunResource updateRun = operation.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created update run: {updateRun.Data.Name}"); #endregion } [Test] [SyncOnly] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task CreateGate() { #region Snippet:CreateGate ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); ArmOperation operation = await fleet .GetContainerServiceFleetGates() .CreateOrUpdateAsync(WaitUntil.Completed, "", gateData); ContainerServiceFleetGateResource gate = operation.Value; Console.WriteLine($"Created gate: {gate.Data.Name}"); #endregion } [Test] [SyncOnly] [Ignore("Only verifying that the sample builds")] public async Task CreateManagedNamespace() { #region Snippet:CreateManagedNamespace ArmClient client = new ArmClient(new DefaultAzureCredential()); `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet` to API version `2025-08-01-preview` (released as v1.2.0-beta.2), adding two new resource types: - `ContainerServiceFleetGateResource` / `ContainerServiceFleetGateCollection` — represents a `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/gates` resource for controlling fleet update progression - `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource` / `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection` — represents a `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/managednamespaces` resource for namespace management within a fleet ## Gaps Found - The README has no Fleet-specific **Key concepts** section — it only links to generic Azure SDK docs; there is no explanation of what a Fleet, Gate, or ManagedNamespace resource is - The README **Examples** section only links to a general code sample gallery; there are no inline or snippet-backed code examples showing how to use Fleet, Gate, or ManagedNamespace operations - No `tests/Samples/` directory exists — there are no test-backed snippet files at all for this package - The **CHANGELOG** entry for v1.2.0-beta.2 (`## 1.2.0-beta.2 (2026-04-06)`) only says "Upgraded api-version to '2025-08-01-preview'" and does not list the new resource types or describe what they enable
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/CHANGELOG.md` Edit the `## 1.2.0-beta.2 (2026-04-06)` entry to describe the newly added resource types: ```markdown ## 1.2.0-beta.2 (2026-04-06) ### Features Added - Upgraded api-version to `2025-08-01-preview`. - Added `ContainerServiceFleetGateResource` and `ContainerServiceFleetGateCollection` for managing fleet update gates (`Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/gates`). - Added `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource` and `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection` for managing namespaces within a fleet (`Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/managednamespaces`). ``` #### File 2: `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md` Replace the **Key concepts** section (currently a one-liner link) and the **Examples** section (currently only a gallery link) with substantive content. Replace the existing `## Key concepts` section with: ```markdown ## Key concepts The `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet` library maps to these top-level resource types in the `Microsoft.ContainerService` provider: | Resource class | ARM resource type | Description | |---|---|---| | `ContainerServiceFleetResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets` | A Fleet hub cluster used to manage a group of member AKS clusters | | `ContainerServiceFleetMemberResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/members` | An AKS cluster enrolled as a member of a Fleet | | `ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRunResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/updateRuns` | An orchestrated update run across member clusters | | `FleetUpdateStrategyResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/updateStrategies` | A reusable update strategy defining stage and group ordering | | `AutoUpgradeProfileResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/autoUpgradeProfiles` | Configuration for automatic Kubernetes version upgrades across member clusters | | `ContainerServiceFleetGateResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/gates` | A gate that can pause an update run until explicitly resolved *(preview)* | | `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceResource` | `Microsoft.ContainerService/fleets/managednamespaces` | A namespace propagated and managed centrally across all member clusters *(preview)* | For background concepts, see the [Kubernetes Fleet Manager documentation]((learn.microsoft.com/redacted) ``` Replace the existing `## Examples` section with: ```markdown ## Examples The following sections show code snippets for common scenarios. `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/` **Triggered by:** PR #57844 by `@frantran` ## What Changed PR #57844 generated version `1.2.0-beta.2` by upgrading the API version to `2025-08-01-preview`. This added two new resource types to the SDK: - **`ContainerServiceFleetGate` / `ContainerServiceFleetGateData` / `ContainerServiceFleetGateCollection`** — Gates support for fleet-level upgrade coordination - **`ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespace` / `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceData` / `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespaceCollection`** — Managed Namespace support Combined with features from `1.2.0-beta.1` (AutoUpgrade profiles, FleetMember labels, TargetKubernetesVersion), the package now has substantially more API surface than what the README describes. ## Gaps Found - The README **Examples** section contains no inline code samples — it only links to `(aka.ms/redacted), which is a generic redirect. There is no package-specific example showing how to create a Fleet, add a member, trigger an update run, create a Gate, or manage a ManagedNamespace. - There are **no snippet placeholders** (`C# Snippet:`) in the README that would be populated by `Update-Snippets.ps1`. - There is **no `tests/Samples/` directory** with hand-crafted sample test files. The only samples are the auto-generated ones under `tests/Generated/Samples/`, which are not wired into the README. - The **Key concepts** section is a one-liner pointing to general SDK docs and says nothing specific to the Fleet service (e.g., what a Fleet is, what Members are, what Update Runs are, what Gates are, what Managed Namespaces are). - The new **Gates** and **ManagedNamespace** features added in this and the prior beta are not mentioned or demonstrated anywhere in the README.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Update `README.md` **File:** `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md` **Action:** Edit Replace the placeholder **Key concepts** section with a concise description of the Fleet service domain model: ```markdown ## Key concepts ### Fleet A `Fleet` resource (represented by `ContainerServiceFleetResource`) is the top-level management object that groups AKS member clusters for coordinated lifecycle management. ### Fleet Member A `ContainerServiceFleetMember` represents an AKS cluster enrolled in the fleet. Members can be labeled for targeted update strategies. ### Update Run A `ContainerServiceFleetUpdateRun` orchestrates rolling upgrades across member clusters in defined stages and groups. ### Update Strategy A `FleetUpdateStrategy` defines a reusable template of stages and groups for update runs. ### Auto Upgrade Profile An `AutoUpgradeProfile` automates Kubernetes version upgrades across fleet members based on release channel settings. ### Gate A `ContainerServiceFleetGate` is a pre/post-upgrade checkpoint that can pause an update run until external conditions are satisfied, enabling integration with deployment pipelines. ### Managed Namespace A `ContainerServiceFleetManagedNamespace` propagates a Kubernetes namespace (with RBAC, resource quotas, and network policies) across all fleet member clusters from the fleet hub. ``` Replace the placeholder **Examples** section content with snippet placeholders (see Step 2): ```markdown ## Examples The following examples demonstrate common operations with the Azure Kubernetes Fleet Manager library. ### Create a Fleet ```C# Snippet:CreateFleet ``` ### Add a Fleet Member ```C# Snippet:AddFleetMember ``` ### Start an Update Run ```C# Snippet:CreateUpdateRun ``` ### Create a Gate ```C# Snippet:CreateGate ``` ### Create a Managed Namespace ```C# Snippet:CreateManagedNamespace ``` For more examples, see the [Azure SDK for .NET management library samples]((aka.ms/redacted) ``` > **Note:** The backtick fences above are abbreviated for clarity. ## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in the `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet` README — missing fleet-specific key concepts, examples, and test-backed snippets — and provides a step-by-step implementation guide to address them.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet` (v1.2.0-beta.2, API version 2025-08-01-preview)
**Affected API:** README / documentation — `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/`
**Scenarios:**
- The "Key concepts" section only links to a generic Azure SDK intro — no description of fleet-specific resource types (Fleet, FleetMember, UpdateRun, UpdateStrategy, AutoUpgradeProfile, Gates)
- The "Examples" section only links to a generic code samples URL — no fleet-specific examples
- No `tests/Samples/` directory exists, so there are no test-backed snippets to wire into the README
- Features from recent releases (AutoUpgrade, Gates, FleetMember labels, TargetKubernetesVersion) are not documented

**Root ask:** Add fleet-specific key concepts, working code snippets for common operations (create fleet, add member, create update run, create AutoUpgrade profile), and wire them into the README via the standard `Update-Snippets.ps1` mechanism.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation gap issue (not a runtime bug). The issue was automatically generated after PR #57925 bumped the version following the 1.2.0-beta.2 release.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Confirm the README at `sdk/fleet/Azure.ResourceManager.ContainerServiceFleet/README.md` still lacks fleet-specific key concepts and examples
2. Verify no `tests/Samples/` directory exists under that service directory
3. Review the implementation guide in the issue body — it provides ready-to-use code for `Sample1_FleetBasicOperations.cs` with four snippets (CreateFleet, AddFleetMember, CreateUpdateRun, CreateAutoUpgradeProfile)
4. Run `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 fleet` after adding the sample file to inject snippets into the README placeholder blocks
5. Run `dotnet build` and `dotnet test --filter TestCategory!=Live` to validate compilation and test execution
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Mgmt` — Package name starts with `Azure.ResourceManager.*`, clearly a management-plane SDK
- **Service:** `Container Service` — Maps to the ContainerServiceFleet service; consistent with similar issues (e.g., #57840, #57857, #57855) tagged with `Container Service`
- **Confidence:** High — Package is explicitly named in the issue title and body; category and service are unambiguous; no alternate labels were plausible
</details>
